title: Child Guidance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Child Guidance 51 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues At Risk Families Child/Adolescent Sex Offenders Mental Health Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details 211INFO 211INFO RESOURCE PARENT SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support, resource referral, and additional after-hours support for foster parents. Eligibility: Any foster parent with an ODHS Child Welfare case or in the Oregon Foster Care System Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email 211 Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR FAMILY DEVELOPMENT MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health assessments and individual, family, and group counseling for children, adolescents, and adults. Addresses concerns ranging from mood changes to more serious mental health conditions, as well as challenges related to life and family transitions. Areas of focus include child abuse, sexual behavior concerns, depression, ADHD, anxiety, and behavioral challenges. Also provides recovery services for SUD and DUI Diversion programs. Offers developmental evaluations for children 0-5. 1234 High Street Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm, evening and weekend availability as needed Email (541) 342-8437 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR FAMILY DEVELOPMENT CENTER FOR FAMILY DEVELOPMENT NORTH BUILDING MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health assessments and individual, family, and group counseling for children, adolescents, and adults. Addresses concerns ranging from mood changes to more serious mental health conditions, as well as challenges related to life and family transitions. Areas of focus include child abuse, sexual behavior concerns, depression, ADHD, anxiety, and behavioral challenges. Provides trauma-informed care that considers physical, psychological, and social factors. Home visits for counseling are available by arrangement. 261 E 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm, evening and weekend availability as needed Email (541) 342-8437 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR FAMILY DEVELOPMENT CENTER FOR FAMILY DEVELOPMENT SOUTH BUILDING MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health assessments and individual, family, and group counseling for children, adolescents, and adults. Home visits for counseling are available by arrangement. 1501 Pearl Street Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm, evening and weekend availability as needed Email (541) 342-8437 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHRISTIANS AS FAMILY ADVOCATES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers Christian faith-based mental health services, including counseling for trauma, adjustment, anger, domestic violence, parenting, marriage, depression, and anxiety; individual and group therapy for children, teens, and adults; and filial, play, and neurofeedback therapy including EEG Cygnet and IASIS microcurrent transcranial. Safe and Sound In protocol (SSP) providers. Provides intervention services to batterers and support to survivors, as well as therapeutic services for children and youth who have been impacted by divorce and/or domestic violence. Offers supervised visitation and parenting classes. 921 Country Club Road Suite 222 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Unrestricted, but cannot work with sex offender population Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 9am-6pm, Wednesday 9:15am-5pm (541) 686-6000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP BEHAVIORAL HEALTHCARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides counseling services for children, adults, and families, as well as medication management, crisis services, and specialized services, including peer support and case management for persons with serious and persistent mental illness. 115 W Bond Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 325-5722 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EUGENE RELIEF NURSERY CHILD AND FAMILY THERAPY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health services for infants, toddlers, and preschool-aged children. Sessions include both the child and their parents or caregivers. Counseling is available in the office, at home, or in the classroom. Focus areas include family therapy, strengthening attachment, and addressing emotional and behavioral concerns. Services are trauma-informed. 1720 W 25th Avenue Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: OHP-covered children age 6 and younger and their families. Priority given to families already enrolled in other Relief Nursery services Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm (541) 343-9706 136 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EUGENE RELIEF NURSERY RELIEF NURSERY ROBIN JAQUA CHILD AND FAMILY CENTER CHILD AND FAMILY THERAPY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health services for infants, toddlers, and preschool-aged children. Offers classes for young children with trained teachers. 300 Jerry's Drive Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 673-4354 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY SOLUTIONS SOUTHERN OREGON FAMILY SOLUTIONS ASHLAND PROCTOR FOSTER CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides individualized community-based treatment for children and youth with severe emotional and behavioral issues. Children and resource (foster) parents are matched, one to two children per home. Children receive individual, group, and family therapy. Skills training, mentoring, case management, and other individualized services offered. Respite care available to foster parents. This is an alternative to BRS residential care, allowing the child to remain in a home environment and community setting. 1836 Fremont Street Ashland, OR 97520 Eligibility: Ages 4-18 who have behavioral issues that require them to be placed in a more specialized foster home with extra support. Must be involved with ODHS Child Welfare. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 916-4777 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY SOLUTIONS SOUTHERN OREGON FAMILY SOLUTIONS GRANTS PASS PROCTOR FOSTER CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides individualized community-based treatment for children and youth with severe emotional and behavioral issues. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 916-4777 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY TREE RELIEF NURSERY ALBANY CLASSROOM FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves families with young children who are experiencing high levels of stress. Provides high-risk families with intensive support through individualized case management. Services include parenting education, support through home visits, group activities, and resource referral. Children participate in therapeutic classrooms with low adult-child ratios, and families receive home visits that promote healthy parenting and child development. 1055 Bain Street Southeast Albany, OR 97322 Eligibility: Birth-5 years old Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 967-6580 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY TREE RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Relief Nurseries serve families with young children who are experiencing high levels of stress. Family Tree Relief Nursery provides high-risk families with intensive support through individualized case management. Risk factors include: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single parent, criminal history, adult victim of child abuse, drug and alcohol history, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, and social isolation. Services include parenting education, support through home visits, group activities, resource referral. Children participate in therapeutic classrooms with low adult-child ratios, and families receive home visits that promote healthy parenting and child development. 1305 Hill Street SE Albany, OR 97322 Eligibility: Birth-5 years old Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 967-6580 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY TREE RELIEF NURSERY LEBANON CLASSROOM FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves families with young children who are experiencing high levels of stress. Therapy is offered to those with chronic and acute issues, as long as there is a specific therapy goal. The clinic is not qualified to provide sex offender treatment or perform custody evaluations. Therapists are current students in the George Fox Doctor of Psychology program. Many are Master's-level trained. A licensed psychologist supervises all therapists. 515 N Villa Road Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Underserved and low-income households, age 4 and older. For individuals under age 18, parental consent is required. Options therapists work together with clients to achieve mutually agreed upon goals. With a comprehensive understanding of the family's history and culture, therapists work closely with clients, in collaboration with their key supports and influences, with the intention of forward movement on a path toward a positive future. Telehealth services are available. Psychiatric care provides comprehensive psychiatric assessments as well as on-going medication management as part of mental health treatment. Clients must be engaged in active therapy services to participate in medication management. 1445 8th Street Florence, OR 97439 Eligibility: Serves children under the age of 18 in Multnomah County and youth under age 24 in Clackamas County.
